invalid URLs like https://.yandex.ru when serializing for persistent

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled

- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit

Display provenance badges (Default/Global) next to module settings fields
so users can see which layer a resolved value comes from.

* feat: §29.3.9 prefetch hints — low-priority module bundle prefetching (#257)

Add PrefetchManager that accepts manifest-level prefetch hints from modules
declaring which other modules they commonly navigate to. Prefetched bundles
are fetched at low priority via requestIdleCallback (setTimeout fallback)
with concurrency control (max 2 concurrent, max 5 queued).

Changes:
- New: prefetchManager.ts — manages loader registry, queue, budget, cancel
- types.ts: add prefetchModules?: string[] to ModuleManifest + DTO + validation
- coreModules.ts: extract import fns for loader registration; Chat prefetches

- ModuleContainer.tsx: trigger prefetch when module reaches Ready/Mounted

- 16 new tests covering queue, dedup, cancel, budget, concurrency, failure

Add frontend-only metrics collector that tracks per-module lifecycle
events (init timing, mount count, suspend/resume, faults) and surfaces
them in the Settings → Modules UI.

New files:

* feat: §29.7.8 memory leak detection in dev mode

* feat: §29.8.3 module visibility per project (#263)

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify
which modules are hidden from the activity bar. Hidden modules remain
accessible via direct URL — only the nav icon is suppressed.

Backend:
- New IModuleVisibilityStore interface (Domain)
- SqliteModuleVisibilityStore with transactional replace (Infrastructure)

- Audit logging for visibility changes

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like

* fix: use authoritative V3 complete text to prevent cross-turn Discord message duplication

The PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer had a race condition caused
by BroadcastingSessionEventSink's fire-and-forget dispatch pattern. When
the assistant.message (complete text) event won the thread pool race and
processed before all deltas, orphaned deltas accumulated in the buffer and
contaminated the next turn's output — producing doubled/corrupt messages.

Fix: when IsCompleteMessage is true, always use the event's authoritative
complete text and discard buffered deltas. This matches what
BroadcastingSessionEventSink already does for message persistence.

Added regression test simulating the race: final processed before deltas,
orphaned deltas present when next turn arrives.

feat(task-state): ADR-038 amendment — bounded cold-start scan + header projection cache (#374)

* feat(task-state): ADR-038 amendment - bounded cold-start scan + metadata projection cache

- Add TaskMetadataHeader/SessionTaskSummary projection (meta_projection.rs)
- Add process-global LRU metadata cache with tiebreak eviction (meta_index.rs, 512 entries)
- Add TaskMetadataRef lazy-load handle, cfg(test)-gated (meta_ref.rs)
- Add StartupBudget with VEX_MAX_STARTUP_TASK_SCANS=200 (config/startup.rs)
- Replace O(N) live_session_task_counts_from with cached metadata scan
- Replace O(N) find_session_task_in_saved_states with metadata-first scan
- Add state_files_from_with_limit() bounded variant
- Delete stale TaskStateLiveSummary / load_live_summary private pair
- Add ADR-038-amendment-2026-04-13.md

Corrects three ADR-038 Extension spec errors: serde early-exit claim,
missing TaskMetadataRef.state field, assert_durable_access bypass.
Uses file.dir (not state_dir_from) for path construction so legacy
search dirs resolve correctly.

* fix: add filesystem-safety validation for agent and team names

Agent and team names are used to derive worktree paths (ADR-034) but
were not validated for path-safety. The existing check enforced
non-empty, length, and uniqueness, yet the doc-comment claimed
filesystem-safe without rejecting path separators, null bytes, control
characters, or the reserved dot names '.' and '..'.

Add is_filesystem_safe() and call it from both validate_agent_names()
and validate_team_members(). Add unit tests covering path separators,
backslashes, control characters, dot/dot-dot names, and a team-name
variant. Existing tests are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden api module against integer overflow from untrusted input

Audit of src/api/ found four integer-overflow paths reachable from
untrusted server data:

- stream.rs process(): buffer-length check used plain addition that
  could wrap on usize overflow; switched to saturating_add.
- stream.rs apply_chat_compat_tool_delta(): tool_call.index arrives
  from deserialized JSON with no upper bound; clamped to 1024 and
  used saturating_add for block_index.
- stream.rs ensure_chat_compat_tool_state(): resize target used
  index + 1 which could wrap to zero, truncating the vector; switched
  to saturating_add.
- client/mod.rs resolve_max_tokens(): float cast (n as f64 * 0.75)
  as u32 lost precision for large context sizes; replaced with exact
  integer arithmetic (n as u64 * 3) / 4.

No unsafe blocks, raw pointers, FFI boundaries, transmute, or
function-pointer casts exist in the api module. The remaining audit
categories (bad character handling, null-terminator assumptions,
stack allocation sizing, control-flow hardening) are clean by design.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden resolve_max_tokens and chat_compat_messages against edge-case panics

resolve_max_tokens: guard clamp(128, ceiling) against panic when a local
server reports n_ctx < 171 (ceiling < 128 violates clamp min <= max
invariant). Return the reduced ceiling directly in that case.

chat_compat_messages: replace unchecked messages.len() + 1 with
saturating_add to prevent theoretical overflow on extreme input sizes.

Add two regression tests covering n_ctx=100 and n_ctx=1 boundary cases.

   - Documented package definition structure and workflow

This maintains all existing boundaries (User/Host/Relation model, no top-level
profile.packages, no new PackageBinding objects) while enabling single-point
package management for future additions.

fix: /scanner → 404 sur Cloudflare Pages + validation PR sur URL de déploiement réelle (#2145)

* Initial plan

* fix: remove dist/404.html for Cloudflare Pages + use deployment URL for PR validation

- Add explicit `rm -f dist/404.html` step before deploying to Cloudflare Pages.
  Vite copies public/404.html (GitHub Pages SPA redirect script) to dist/404.html,
  and Cloudflare Pages serves it with a 404 status for unknown routes like /scanner,
  preventing the `_redirects /* /index.html 200` catch-all from running.
  The postbuild script (setup-github-pages-spa.mjs) also does this, but this
  workflow step is an explicit, visible safety net.

- Fix PR validation to use the actual deployment URL instead of the static
  production alias. Previously, PR events checked https://akiprisaye-web.pages.dev
  (which could be stale/broken), causing all PRs to fail even with correct fixes.
  The validate script already handles Cloudflare Access protected previews
  gracefully (isCloudflareAccessPage → skipped with warning, not hard failure).

commit.message:
v0.2.55: Fix timezone bug — resolve TZ from HA Supervisor API at startup

At startup, app now resolves timezone via:
1. TZ env var
2. /data/options.json 'timezone' option
3. HA Core /api/config time_zone via Supervisor API
Applies os.environ['TZ'] + time.tzset() so date.today() uses local time.
Added 'timezone' option to config.json schema for manual override.
